title: Money - The Kathmandu Post
authors: By Ajit Tiwari, Shiva Puri & Madhav Dhungana
summary: Money In Sindhuli, cows are connected By Sangam Prasain Sensor-based monitoring of high-yield Holsteins is boosting milk output, cutting labour and transforming livelihoods in Kamalamai. Rs100 tax rule aimed at curbing small-scale smuggling hits daily shoppers at Nepal-India border By Ajit Tiwari, Shiva Puri & Madhav Dhungana Strict checks on goods valued above Rs100 at Nepal-India crossings draw
content:
Money In Sindhuli, cows are connected By Sangam Prasain Sensor-based monitoring of high-yield Holsteins is boosting milk output, cutting labour and transforming livelihoods in Kamalamai. Rs100 tax rule aimed at curbing small-scale smuggling hits daily shoppers at Nepal-India border By Ajit Tiwari, Shiva Puri & Madhav Dhungana Strict checks on goods valued above Rs100 at Nepal-India crossings draw anger and complaints in border districts. Nepal business delegation pitches ‘hydro-to-data’ vision in Washington meetings By Post Report The US Trade and Development Agency expressed interest in potentially supporting feasibility studies for such projects, according to the delegation. Despite surging costs, Everest still draws climbers as China’s north side stays shut By Sangam Prasain The Department of Tourism has issued Everest climbing permits to 360 individuals as of Friday, compared with 331 during the same period last year. Selective buying lifts market amid flat benchmark By Pritam Bhattarai Hotel sector gains 5.13 percent on seasonal optimism linked to New Year demand and two-day weekend impact. Global ride-hailing giant Uber set for Nepal’s already crowded market By Krishana Prasain US firm plans debut within a month as interim rules hastened amid law delays. Loans are cheaper than ever. Borrowers are still holding back By Yagya Banjade With banks flush with funds but credit demand subdued, mounting excess liquidity points to deeper signs of a liquidity trap in Nepal’s economy. Interim standards in the works as law to regulate ride-hailing sector is delayed By Krishana Prasain Draft guidelines aim to regulate the booming sector amid years of legal delays and court pressure. Nepse allows round-the-clock order placement as trading rules revised By Yagya Banjade Sebon also approves changes to price bands, pre-open limits and circuit breaker system. Petroleum prices increased for fifth time in 31 days By Pritam Bhattarai Diseal, kerosene and aviation fuel prices increase by Rs 30 and Rs 5 per litre, respectively. NOC cites rising global oil prices and West Asia tensions behind repeated adjustments. Nepal ride-hailing law fails to take off despite court orders By Krishana Prasain Six years after judicial directives, delays persist as Bagmati cites political changes. Riders, companies and commuters face growing risks and disputes. Hormuz Strait closure takes its toll on global economic growth By Krishna Acharya Middle East conflict casts shadow over global economy. Bullion prices rebound after early-week dip By Pritam Bhattarai Gold and silver recover from sharp declines to close the week higher. Nepali media is fighting for survival. Its journalists are fighting for their paychecks By Daya Dudraj As the industry faces an existential threat, newsroom staff are going months without pay, with little recourse and nowhere else to go. Fluctuations mark Nepal real estate over past three years By Krishana Prasain Central bank’s first market report shows strong demand, regional shifts, and cautious recovery in revenues. Nepali airlines in a scramble as fuel costs spike, passengers vanish By Sangam Prasain Buddha Air says daily passenger numbers have plunged by 1,500, and flights cut by 25 percent. Buddha Air relaunches Royal Club as fully digital loyalty programme By Post Report Under the new system, passengers can accumulate mileage points from flights and redeem them as discounts on future bookings. Chinese planes continue to be a costly burden for Nepal Airlines By Sangam Prasain Grounded for years, the aircraft continue to drain millions as efforts to sell, lease, or return them fail amid financial and diplomatic constraints. Prime Minister Shah cites social media as income source By Post Report Shah has four million followers in Facebook, 1.2 million in YouTube and half million followers in X. Provinces urged to strengthen cooperation in agriculture sector By Post Report Experts and officials highlight policy gaps, budget constraints and the need for coordinated action at the interprovincial level.
